methodology: Platform-product OKRs with explicit customer outcomes and team ownership.
description: Preparation Objectives for the next quarter.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe platform outcomes for internal customers, not internal feature checklists.
Key results emphasize adoption, economics, controls, and reliability.
Every key result names executing and supporting teams.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Scale self-service without scaling platform support work
companyObjectives.statement: Prepare the next quarter around platform leverage: more teams should complete core workflows on their own while enablement load stays bounded.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Ephemeral environment economics and adoption discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shapes where self-service should expand and where it should stay narrower.

keyResults.target: Make the most frequent onboarding and runtime workflows successfully self-service
keyResults.baseline: Teams still rely on enablement and support on several high-frequency actions
keyResults.statement: Increase self-service completion by tightening portal flows, template quality, and policy-aware runtime automation.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Scale self-service without scaling platform support work

executingTeams.intent: Own the user-facing workflow improvements that raise successful self-service completion.

supportingTeams.intent: Raise template quality and path coverage for common team needs.
Improve runtime workflows so the most common environment actions succeed without manual intervention.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: Requires measurement and iterative workflow improvement rather than fixed feature scope.

keyResults.metric: Platform support load per active team
keyResults.target: Reduce support intensity while the number of active platform teams grows
keyResults.baseline: Support demand still scales too directly with new onboarding and exception handling
keyResults.statement: Reduce support load per team by productizing repeated migration and exception pain points.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Scale self-service without scaling platform support work

executingTeams.intent: Cluster repeated onboarding and migration pain points into platform-product improvements.

supportingTeams.intent: Quantify recurring support demand and identify the highest-leverage pain points to productize.
Productize the most common exception classes instead of handling them repeatedly through manual review.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: The right mix of workflow, policy, and product changes should remain discoverable through customer evidence.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Make platform value and cost visible enough to govern as a product portfolio
companyObjectives.statement: Prepare the next quarter around platform economics and portfolio clarity so teams can make better investment, adoption, and retirement decisions.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Ephemeral environment economics and adoption discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Highlights the need to join flow metrics with cost and usage evidence.

keyResults.target: Create a weekly decision pack that joins adoption, flow, reliability, and cost
keyResults.baseline: Platform reporting remains split across delivery, cost, and reliability views
keyResults.statement: Publish a trusted weekly platform value pack so product, engineering, and finance leaders can prioritize the platform portfolio on evidence.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Make platform value and cost visible enough to govern as a product portfolio

executingTeams.intent: Own the shared metrics model and weekly decision pack.

supportingTeams.intent: Provide incident and reliability signals required for platform portfolio decisions.
Provide service and ownership metadata used to segment the platform estate.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: Needs iterative refinement of metrics and leadership use cases.

keyResults.metric: Shared-foundation reuse across specialist platform teams
keyResults.target: Clarify where specialist platforms can reuse common contracts without fragmentation
keyResults.baseline: Specialist teams still duplicate some foundational runtime, delivery, and telemetry patterns
keyResults.statement: Increase shared-foundation reuse by documenting boundaries, contracts, and supported extension points for specialist platform teams.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Make platform value and cost visible enough to govern as a product portfolio

executingTeams.intent: Own the shared runtime and integration contracts used by specialist platform teams.

supportingTeams.intent: Document product boundaries, dependencies, and ownership in the platform portfolio.
Bring specialist-platform migration and adoption evidence into the product decisions.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: Requires product-boundary decisions and migration evidence rather than a single architecture change.
